text.title: Founder Deal Kill Rule Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding the rule-based deal-kill checklist. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every kill condition MUST be numeric: no_reply_days > X, last_meeting_days > Y, missed_milestone_count >= 1, budget_confirmed=false at stage gate, champion_left=true. "Feels stale" is rejected.
rule.rationale: Qualitative kill drives emotional reluctance to defer the call indefinitely.

rule.statement: When ≥2 kill conditions trip on the same deal, the deal MUST move to "dead" stage with reason logged. No per-deal debate during the weekly review.
rule.rationale: Founders kill 5x more deals when the rule decides for them than when "we discuss it".

rule.statement: Killed deals are reversible within 30 days if the prospect returns with a clear next-step + named champion. After 30 days, re-qualification (new opportunity) is required.
rule.rationale: Reversibility reduces founder anxiety enough to make rule application stick; the 30-day cap prevents zombies.

rule.statement: The weekly review processes ALL kill candidates as one batch. No per-deal "yes but..." conversation. Edge cases tagged "escalate" for end-of-batch review.
rule.rationale: Per-deal debate restores the emotional barrier the rule exists to bypass.

rule.statement: Quarterly retro MUST review the kill log: deals killed that later reopened, deals kept that should have been killed. Thresholds updated based on retro evidence.
rule.rationale: Without retro, thresholds drift and the rule loses credibility.
